ethernet.page_description: View detailed information about your device's Ethernet status, including connection status, speed, and other relevant metrics.
ethernet.card_description: Current Ethernet link status, speed, and duplex settings.
ethernet.error_load_failed: Could not load ethernet status from the device.
ethernet.toast_error_set_speed: Failed to set link speed limit

ethernet_leds.card_description: Enable or disable Wake-on-LAN on the Ethernet port. When enabled, another device on the network can power this unit out of sleep by sending a magic packet. Leave disabled if you don't use it — most always-on modem gateways have no need for it.
ethernet_leds.applying: Applying… your connection may briefly drop ({{seconds}}s)

dns.page_description: Override carrier-assigned DNS servers with your preferred resolvers for all devices on the local network.
dns.card_description: Override carrier DNS servers for all devices on your local network.
dns.error_at_least_one: Enter at least one DNS server address
dns.toast_error_apply: Failed to apply DNS settings — check connection

ttl_mtu.page_description: Set custom Time To Live, Hop Limit, and Maximum Transmission Unit values on the cellular data interface.

ttl.card_description: Set custom TTL (IPv4) and Hop Limit (IPv6) values applied to outbound packets on the cellular interface.
ttl.alert_profile_override: TTL/HL configuration is managed by the {{profileName}} Custom SIM Profile.
ttl.error_at_least_one: Enter at least a TTL or Hop Limit value
ttl.toast_error_apply: Failed to apply TTL/Hop Limit settings

mtu.card_description: Set the maximum packet size on the cellular data interface. Lower values can help with fragmentation issues.

ippt.page_description: Assign the modem's public IP directly to a connected device, bypassing the router's NAT layer.
ippt.card_description: Assign the modem's public IP directly to a downstream device, bypassing the router's NAT.
ippt.helper_mac_manual: Enter the MAC address of the device that will receive the WAN IP.
ippt.toast_error_apply: Failed to save IP Passthrough settings
ippt.dialog_desc_reboot_notice: Applying these changes will save the configuration and immediately reboot the device.
ippt.dialog_desc_unreachable_warning: Once IP Passthrough is active, the device's local gateway will no longer be reachable. Make sure you have an active Tailscale connection or another out-of-band method to access the device after reboot.

masquerade.page_description: Make all HTTPS traffic appear as a whitelisted service to carrier DPI
masquerade.card_description: Make HTTPS traffic appear as a whitelisted service to carrier DPI.
masquerade.card_description_full: Make HTTPS traffic appear as a whitelisted service to carrier DPI by injecting fake TLS handshakes with a spoofed domain. Some carriers may detect this behavior and de-prioritize your connection.
masquerade.error_binary_not_installed: The `nfqws` binary is not installed on this device.
masquerade.error_install_from_video_optimizer: Install it from the <link>Video Optimizer</link> page, then check again.
masquerade.alert_video_optimizer_active: Video Optimizer is currently active. Disable it first before enabling Traffic Masquerade.
masquerade.alert_kernel_module_missing: Required kernel module not found. Install with opkg install kmod-nft-queue on the device.
masquerade.helper_domain: The domain that appears in the fake TLS handshake sent to the carrier.
masquerade.section_remove_binary_desc: Uninstall the nfqws binary from this device.
masquerade.dialog_uninstall_desc: This will remove the nfqws binary and disable both Video Optimizer and Traffic Masquerade. You can reinstall it from the Video Optimizer page.
masquerade.test_description: Make an HTTPS request to a CDN and verify that fake SNI packets are being injected by comparing nftables counters before and after

video_optimizer.page_description: Bypass carrier video throttling using DPI evasion on targeted video CDN hostnames
video_optimizer.card_description: Bypass carrier video throttling on cellular connections using DPI evasion.
video_optimizer.card_description_full: Bypass carrier video throttling on cellular connections using DPI evasion. Some carriers (e.g., T-Mobile) may detect DPI evasion and de-prioritize your connection.
video_optimizer.error_binary_not_installed: The `nfqws` binary is not installed on this device.
video_optimizer.error_download_zapret: Download it automatically from the <link>zapret</link> project.
video_optimizer.alert_masquerade_active: Traffic Masquerade is currently active. Disable it first before enabling Video Optimizer.
video_optimizer.alert_kernel_module_missing: Required kernel module not found. Install with opkg install kmod-nft-queue on the device.
video_optimizer.help_desync_repeats: Number of times the DPI-evading packet is sent for each matched flow. Higher values improve reliability when packets are lost or reordered, but increase CPU use and upstream bandwidth. Default 1 works for most users.
video_optimizer.aria_desync_repeats_info: More information about DPI Desync Repeats
video_optimizer.section_remove_binary_desc: Uninstall the nfqws binary from this device.
video_optimizer.dialog_uninstall_desc: This will remove the nfqws binary and disable both Video Optimizer and Traffic Masquerade. You can reinstall it later from this page.
video_optimizer.verify_description: Confirm DPI evasion is intercepting traffic for hostlisted domains
video_optimizer.hostlist_card_description: Hostnames targeted for DPI evasion — video CDN domains that will have their TLS handshakes modified
video_optimizer.toast_import_no_new: No new domains to import — all already in list
video_optimizer.dialog_restore_desc: This will replace the current list with the factory default CDN hostnames. Any custom domains you added will be removed.
video_optimizer.validation_domain_needs_dot: Must contain at least one dot
